text: Consider bootstrap stub that delegates to tracker-branch tool

current vendoring model (clone to tools/gittoc/) is working well and is simple to understand. The bootstrap-stub architecture would reduce visible-branch churn but adds indirection and complexity. Only worth revisiting if the vendored-copy model causes real pain (e.g. updating gittoc across many repos becomes a burden).
Related bootstrap work: see T-81 for the user-facing single-command bootstrap flow. T-35 is narrower and more conceptual (stub/delegation), so triage together before implementation.
